data.title: Faraday Future soars to a 6-month high as shorts feel the heat
data.description:
Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (NASDAQ:FFAI) shot up 29.2% in early Thursday morning trading. The electric vehicle maker did not release a new press release or SEC filing to account for the buying action. 

Notably, short interest on the EV stock stands at 25.3% of the total float, which is the second largest short position in the automobile industry behind only Lucid Group (LCID). 

For its part, Faraday Future (NASDAQ:FFAI) said this week that it has identified potential illegal short selling and the deliberate dissemination of false or misleading information aimed at disrupting the market. The company noted that it has issued a legal letter to the parties involved and reserves all rights to pursue legal remedies.

The rally for Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (FFAI) pushed shares up to their highest level since early January.

data.title: A. O. Smith shares rise as Q2 earnings beat estimates on North America strength
data.description:
Shares of A. O. Smith (NYSE:AOS) rose 6.8% as regular trading opened Thursday after the company reported second-quarter earnings that beat estimates.

The Milwaukee-based water-technology company posted revenue of $1.01 billion for the quarter ended June 30, a 1% year-over-year decline, in line with the Wall Street estimate of $1 billion. Earnings came in at $1.07 a share, topping the consensus estimate of $1.

While commercial boiler sales remained strong and North America margins improved to 25.4%, overall sales were weighed down by a double-digit decline in China. The company’s “Rest of World” segment saw sales fall 2%, driven by an 11% drop in China revenue (in local currency), which offset a 19% increase in India and $16 million in contributions from the company’s Pureit acquisition.

New Chief Executive Steve Shafer acknowledged the China challenges and announced that the company has launched a formal strategic review of the business there, including potential partnerships or other structural changes.

“We believe the China market has substantial long-term prospects,” Shafer said in a statement, without offering a timeline for the evaluation.

In North America, sales dipped 1% to $779 million, as higher boiler demand was offset by lower residential water heater volumes. The company noted that productivity initiatives helped smooth out some pre-buy distortions tied to pricing and tariffs.

Raising lower end of guidance

A. O. Smith (NYSE:AOS) raised its full-year outlook. It now expects 2025 revenue to grow 1% to 3% and earnings of $3.70 to $3.90 a share, upping the lower end of prior guidance of $3.60 to $3.90 a share. The company repurchased $251 million in stock in the first half of the year and plans to return $400 million to shareholders in buybacks by year-end.

data.title: Gentherm outlines path to $300M lumbar and massage growth by 2027 while expanding commercial vehicle wins
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Gentherm Incorporated (THRM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • William T. Presley, President and CEO, outlined that "the Gentherm team delivered results in line with our expectations in a highly dynamic environment. We improved adjusted EBITDA margin performance versus the first quarter by more than 100 basis points and achieved another strong quarter of automotive new business awards with over $600 million secured in Q2 and $1 billion year-to-date." He highlighted a significant new contract with Ford, stating Gentherm secured "all of the heat, ventilation, lumbar and massage systems on Ford's next-generation F-150, F-250 and F-350 platforms, making us the full comfort solution provider on one of the most significant platforms in the market." Presley emphasized progress in Asia, noting that "70% of our Chinese awards were with Chinese domestic OEMs compared to 50% over the last 2 years," with a strategic aim to shift the China customer mix closer to the overall market.
  • Presley discussed product growth, stating, "Pneumatic, lumbar and massage adoption rates are accelerating. In 2024, the product line grew more than 20% from the prior year. And year-to-date, we've grown more than 15%. We expect that trend to continue. We project that our lumbar and massage product line will grow from approximately $175 million in 2024 to well over $300 million by 2027."
  • Presley also described the company’s expansion into adjacent markets, reporting five new awards in the quarter, including two new commercial vehicle programs and three valve awards for powersports platforms.
  • Jonathan C. Douyard, Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer, stated, "For the second quarter, we secured $620 million of automotive new business awards... We delivered $45.9 million of adjusted EBITDA in the quarter or 12.2% of sales compared to 13.3% in the second quarter of last year. The decrease was primarily driven by higher material costs, which includes unfavorable product mix as well as higher labor costs and expenses related to our footprint realignment." He added, "Adjusted diluted earnings per share in the quarter were $0.54 per share compared to $0.66 per share in the second quarter of last year."

Outlook

  • Douyard announced, "We have updated our guidance and increased the revenue midpoint. We now expect revenue to be in the range of $1.43 billion to $1.5 billion. On EBITDA, given our results year-to-date, increased clarity on the impact of tariffs based on what is in place today as well as the expectation of improved performance in the second half, we have narrowed the adjusted EBITDA margin range to 11.7% to 12.5%." He added, "We expect third quarter overall results to be roughly in line with the second quarter despite industry reports suggesting a mid-single-digit decrease in light vehicle production sequentially."

Financial Results

  • Douyard reported, "Second quarter revenue decreased 0.2% compared to the same period last year. Foreign exchange adjusted revenues decreased 1.6%. Automotive, Climate and Comfort Solutions revenue increased 3.8% year-over-year or 2.5% ex FX, which partially offset planned revenue decreases from previously discussed strategic exits. Medical revenue decreased 3.8% year-over-year or 4.8%, excluding the impact of FX." He also noted, "Our balance sheet remains strong, and we have generated $32 million of operating cash flow year-to-date. We repurchased $10 million of shares in the second quarter. Overall net debt stood at $81 million, while our net leverage ratio remained flat at 0.5 turns. Our available liquidity was $416 million at the end of the quarter, an increase of $15 million from the prior year."

Q&A

  • Ryan Ronald Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum: "You mentioned Q3 expected to be similar to Q2 results despite the industry decline of mid-single digits. Is that a company-specific outperformance you expect given the visibility from new launches?" Douyard: "We do have a number of new launches that are going to come out in the third quarter really across the regions...the visibility we have here in the short term solidifies our view that Q3 looks a lot like Q2 from a top line perspective and expect the P&L to follow."
  • Sigdahl: "Can you compare and contrast...current versus the next gen F-Series?" Presley: "That was continuation really of our current platform. So similar content as previous F-Series. It just solidifies the desire of the customer to have our features because of the value that it provides to the end user."
  • Sigdahl: "Just on the adjacencies, I believe you called out 3 powersports platforms that you have awards. Anything you can expand on that?" Presley: "I can't expand on who the OEM is. I'll tell you that they're UTV-style vehicles...it just proves the thesis that our valves really being a, what I call a catalog component, read across to other industries...we think this is a great opportunity for us in the future."
  • Ryan Joseph Brinkman, JPMorgan: "On the commercial vehicles, are these commercial trucks?...Did you mention another end market, too?" Presley: "The commercial vehicle wins that we're talking about here are specifically thermal solutions, and there were 2 awards on commercial vehicle. One of them is actually a heavy truck, so a Class A over-the-road truck...The other one is a last mile delivery van. The valves specifically were in the powersports platforms that was separate of any thermal and pneumatic solution. The other end markets that we specifically mentioned were 2-wheelers...and then motion furniture."
  • Matthew Butler Koranda, ROTH: "To hit the midpoint of the guide on EBITDA, it does look like in the second half, you do need to show a little bit of EBITDA margin expansion...Should we expect that in the third quarter? Or is that more of a fourth quarter event?" Douyard: "We expect the third quarter to look a lot like the second quarter did. And so it should be similar from an EBITDA margin perspective. We will see an increase as we get into the fourth quarter, and that would be where we see more of the margin expansion."
  • Koranda: "You mentioned sort of one of the reasons for the underperformance relative to production is kind of lower exposure to the China domestic OEMs...how long it may take before you're consistently outperforming production in China over time?" Presley: "By next year, we're at that 60-40 customer split. So 60% global, 40% domestic. So that starts improving actually for us next year...our plan is to represent the market there sometime in the next 18 to 24 months."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions were inquisitive but constructive, with a focus on the sustainability of guidance, the Ford contract, adjacent market expansion, and the China strategy. Tone remained neutral to slightly positive, with analysts probing for details on operational execution and strategic direction.
  • Management’s tone during prepared remarks was confident and emphasized strategic wins and growth targets. In the Q&A, management was consistent and provided detailed, direct answers, expressing confidence in operational improvements and strategic execution.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts displayed increased confidence. Management used phrases such as "we expect that trend to continue" and "we are acting with a sense of urgency," while analysts’ tone was less focused on risks and more on execution and growth initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q2 call featured a raised revenue midpoint in guidance, while the previous quarter maintained the prior range amid industry uncertainty. Q2 saw a focus on specific new business wins and a detailed update on adjacent market penetration, while Q1 centered more on operational foundation and early-stage expansion efforts.
  • Key metrics saw sequential improvement in adjusted EBITDA margin and strong new business awards, up from $400 million in Q1 to $620 million in Q2. The China domestic OEM strategy showed acceleration, with a higher percentage of awards versus Q1.
  • Management's tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q1 to more assertive confidence in Q2, backed by concrete wins and product adoption rates. Analysts' focus shifted from risk and guidance resilience in Q1 to growth execution and specifics on new markets in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited higher material and labor costs, unfavorable product mix, and expenses related to footprint realignment as margin pressures. Douyard highlighted, "we experienced timing disconnects between tariff expense and recovery as well as dilution from the pass-through revenue, which collectively impacted margins by approximately 15 basis points in the quarter."
  • Presley acknowledged underperformance in Asia and outlined the strategy to shift the China customer mix toward domestic OEMs, aiming for a 60-40 split by next year. The need to close the revenue gap in Asia remains a focus.
  • Analyst concerns centered on the timing and sustainability of margin improvements, execution in adjacent markets, and the pace of China progress. Management addressed these with specific action plans and near-term targets.

Final Takeaway

Gentherm’s second quarter highlighted notable wins in automotive and adjacent markets, increased guidance for full-year revenue, and a clear strategic plan to accelerate growth in its lumbar and massage product line, targeting well over $300 million by 2027. Management emphasized strong new business awards, operational improvements, and expanding relationships with Chinese domestic OEMs, while reaffirming its commitment to margin expansion and shareholder value creation amid ongoing cost and market challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Performance Shipping signs refinancing agreement with Alpha Bank
data.description:
Performance Shipping (NASDAQ:PSHG) Thursday announced that it has signed an agreement for the refinancing of its existing loan facility with Alpha Bank S.A. through two separate wholly-owned subsidiaries of the company.

This facility refinances the full outstanding loan balance of $29.8 million previously secured by the M/T P. Long Beach and the M/T P. Aliki.

This agreement follows the acceptance of a commitment letter from Alpha Bank, as previously announced on June 24.

The facility bears interest at the rate of SOFR plus 1.90% per annum and will be repayable in twenty consecutive quarterly installments of $1,050,000 each, with a balloon payment of $8,750,000 payable concurrent with the twentieth quarterly installment in mid-2030.

data.title: Marine Max slashes guidance as consumers delay purchases
data.description:
Shares of Marine Max (NYSE:HZO) limped into Thursday’s open after gloomy fiscal third quarter results led the world’s largest boat and yacht retailer to slash its full year profit outlook significantly below market expectations.

“A combination of ongoing economic uncertainty, evolving trade policies and geopolitical tensions contributed to weak retail demand across the recreational marine industry in the June quarter,” said Brett McGill, CEO of MarineMax, adding that the challenging business conditions is leading consumers to delay purchases until conditions improve.

Although the company does expect market conditions to improve in the second half of the year thanks to new tax legislation, easing geopolitical tensions, and potential trade deals, Marine Max (NYSE:HZO) cut its profit guidance by half at the midpoint to $0.45 to $0.95 per share from prior guidance of $1.40 to $2.40 and is nearly $0.50 below Wall Street’s estimates at the midpoint.

Adjusted EBITDA is now forecasted to be within a range of $105M to $120M, versus earlier guidance of $140M to $170M.

For the reported quarter, Marine Max (NYSE:HZO) earned an adjusted profit of $0.49 per share, a 68% deterioration from the same quarter last year. Total revenue fell 13% to a below consensus $657.2M, as comparable store sales were down 9%. However, thanks to diversification efforts and contributions from high-margin areas like superyacht sales, marinas, finance and insurance, the company’s profit margin declined by only 160 basis points to 30.4%.

Coupled with disappointing results from Brunswick (BC) and a 16% drop in Marine Max (HZO) shares, OneWater Marine (ONEW), Malibu Boats (MBUU), MasterCraft Boat Holdings (MCFT), Polaris (PII), and BRP Inc (DOOO) all lower in sympathy.

data.title: Rheinmetall attracts multiple suitors for civilian division, CEO confirms
data.description:
Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) has drawn interest from seven potential buyers for its civilian business, Chief Executive Armin Papperger said to reporters Thursday during a visit to the company’s facility in Unterlüß, Bloomberg News reported.

Papperger said any sale would hinge on a fair valuation and strong job security for employees.

The German defense contractor has made clear that its civilian division, primarily focused on automotive technology, is no longer considered central to its strategic vision. Profit margins in that segment have lagged far behind those in Rheinmetall’s rapidly growing defense arm, prompting the company to repurpose some plants, such as those in Neuss and Berlin, from civilian to military production.

One Equity Partners has emerged as a potential bidder, according to an earlier report from Handelsblatt.

Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) isn’t alone in rethinking its industrial portfolio. Renk Group (OTCPK:RNKGF), a key supplier of transmissions for the Leopard 2 tank and a Rheinmetall collaborator, is also reportedly exploring strategic options for its civilian business, including the possibility of a partial divestment, as reported by Bloomberg News.

data.title: Algoma Steel to seek $500M from Canadian government in emergency trade war relief
data.description:
Algoma Steel (NASDAQ:ASTL) -2.9% in early trading Thursday after saying it is seeking $500M in support from Canada's federal government, as financial pressures mount from U.S. tariffs on Canadian steel.

The company said it applied for the funding under the Large Enterprise Tariff Loan program, announced by the federal government in March to support companies affected by the tariffs.

Algoma (NASDAQ:ASTL) said it has sufficient resources on hand to manage its liquidity over the near term, but is considering various alternatives to bolster liquidity while exploring diversification of the company's customer base to facilitate long-term competitiveness.

President Trump imposed 25% tariffs on global imports of steel into the U.S. in March, which he doubled to 50% last month, which essentially closed the U.S. market to Canadian steel producers, and Prime Minister Carney has said a tariff-free trade deal between Canada and the U.S. is not realistic.

Algoma Steel (ASTL) has outsized exposure to the tariffs because of its single Ontario plant that relies heavily on the U.S. market, and the company also is Canada's only manufacturer of steel plate used in the defense industry; before the trade war, Algoma sold ~60% of its production to the U.S.

data.title: Verastem Oncology gets FDA fast track status for cancer drug
data.description:
Verastem Oncology (NASDAQ:VSTM) said that the U.S. FDA has granted fast track designation to its experimental drug candidate, VS-7375, as a treatment for pancreatic cancer.

The designation is specifically given for the initial treatment of patients with KRAS G12D-mutated advanced or spreading pancreatic cancer, as well as for those who have already undergone at least one standard treatment. 

Fast-track designation allows a faster timeline for the development and review of drugs targeted at serious conditions with unmet medical needs, and enables developers to communicate frequently with the regulator on plans for clinical studies.
